title: Apply Now: National Service Trainee (Community Development and CSR) – Accra, Ghana
summary: he National Service with Operations (Community Development Programme) offers an exciting opportunity for graduates in Ghana to gain practical, hands-on experience in community development and digital skills training. Based in Accra within the Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) department, this role is designed to equip National Service personnel with essential professional skills while contributing to impactful initiatives that promote digital inclusion among young people. The trainee will play a key role in supporting programmes such as Coding for Kids (C4K), coding competitions, mentorship initiatives, and broader community outreach efforts. This position blends programme management, stakeholder engagement, monitoring and evaluation, and communications—making it ideal for individuals seeking a well-rounded early-career experience. Role Details Location: Accra, Ghana Department: Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) Working Hours: 40 hours per week Job Description As a National Service Trainee, you will support the planning, coordination, implementation, and monitoring of community-based digital skills programmes. You will collaborate with schools, volunteers, partners, and internal teams to ensure successful delivery and measurable impact. This role provides a unique opportunity to build experience in programme coordination, communications, stakeholder engagement, and event management, all while contributing to meaningful social impact initiatives. Key Responsibilities Programme Coordination Implementation You will assist in organizing and executing community development initiatives aimed at improving digital literacy and skills among young learners. This includes coordinating with partner schools, managing programme schedules, and supporting logistics for events such as coding competitions, exhibitions, bootcamps, and outreach programmes. Monitoring, Evaluation Reporting The role involves collecting and compiling programme data, including attendance records, assessments, and participant feedback. You will support the preparation of reports and impact summaries, track key performance indicators (KPIs), and document success stories that highlight programme achievements. Stakeholder Partner Engagement You will help maintain strong relationships with schools, teachers, parents, volunteers, and partners. Responsibilities include assisting with onboarding processes, preparing presentations and partnership materials, and supporting outreach to potential donors and sponsors. Communications Content Support The trainee will contribute to communication efforts by drafting social media content, documenting programme activities through photos and reports, and supporting newsletters and CSR updates. This helps amplify the impact and visibility of the programmes. Administrative Support You will assist with documentation management, meeting coordination, and logistical arrangements for field visits and programme activities. This ensures smooth day-to-day operations and effective team collaboration. Qualifications To be eligible for this opportunity, applicants must: Hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Education, Social Sciences, Business Administration, or a related field Be eligible to undertake National Service in Ghana Preferred Skills (Nice to Have) While not mandatory, the following skills will strengthen your application: Strong organizational and coordination abilities Excellent written and verbal communication skills Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Basic knowledge of digital tools and programming (e.g., Scratch, HTML, Python) Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines Strong interpersonal and stakeholder engagement skills Passion for youth development and digital inclusion Perks and Benefits Participants in this programme will enjoy a range of benefits designed to support both professional and personal growth: Competitive monthly allowance Provision of a workstation (laptop, external monitor, etc.) End-of-year package Participation in AmaliTechies bonding activities, including bi-monthly social events and sports activities Additional Information AmaliTech is committed to inclusivity and encourages applications from Persons with Disabilities (PWDs). Applicants who require assistance during the application process are encouraged to contact the HR team via email at recruitment@amalitech.com , providing details on the support needed. Recruitment Process The selection process is structured to assess both technical and practical capabilities: General application (click “I’m Interested” to apply) Assessment (Simulation Project) Online interview(s) Job offer How to Apply Interested and qualified applicants should follow the application process by clicking the “Apply Now” button and selecting “I’m Interested.” Required Documents Ensure you prepare the following documents before applying: Latest CV (PDF format) Academic and professional certificates CLICK HERE TO APPLY What to Expect Joining AmaliTech offers a supportive and diverse work environment where innovation and collaboration are encouraged. As a National Service Trainee, you will gain valuable exposure to real-world projects, work alongside a talented and welcoming team, and develop skills that can launch your career in the global IT and development space. This opportunity is ideal for graduates looking to combine technical knowledge with social impact, while building a strong foundation for future career growth. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: National Service Trainee (Community Development and CSR) – Accra, Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for OceanX Ocean Futures Fellowship 2026 (Paid Remote Program for Ocean Professionals)
summary: The Ocean Futures Fellowship, offered by OceanX , is a forward-looking professional development program designed to equip emerging ocean professionals with the skills, experience, and global exposure needed to address some of the most urgent challenges facing the world’s oceans. This six-month, full-time fellowship provides a unique combination of technical training, mentorship, and hands-on project work, all delivered in a fully remote format. At its core, the fellowship aims to build sustainable career pathways in ocean-related fields while fostering a new generation of leaders committed to ocean conservation, innovation, and public engagement. Participants benefit from OceanX’s extensive international network, gaining access to interdisciplinary collaborations that span science, media, and technology. The program is closely aligned with global sustainability priorities, contributing to the objectives of the United Nations ’s Ocean Decade and Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water). Through this alignment, the fellowship emphasizes the importance of ocean literacy, storytelling, and scientific advancement in protecting marine ecosystems. Fellows also receive a monthly stipend and may have the rare opportunity to travel aboard the OceanXplorer , OceanX’s state-of-the-art research and media vessel, depending on program needs and individual performance. Fellowship Focus Areas The Ocean Futures Fellowship offers six specialized tracks, each tailored to different professional interests and skill sets. Applicants are expected to demonstrate experience or strong interest in at least one area, alongside a working knowledge of artificial intelligence (AI) and responsible use of emerging technologies. 1. AI Fellow AI Fellows work at the intersection of technology and ocean science. They design and implement multimodal AI systems in collaboration with OceanX teams to accelerate scientific insights and enhance storytelling. This role is ideal for individuals interested in applying machine learning, data science, and AI tools to real-world environmental challenges. 2. Digital Correspondent Fellow Digital Correspondent Fellows focus on communicating ocean science and conservation through compelling storytelling. Using journalism and digital platforms, they translate complex scientific findings into engaging narratives that can reach global audiences. 3. Digital Asset Archives Fellow This role supports the organization and management of OceanX’s extensive media archive. Fellows help maintain, catalog, and improve access to a growing collection of scientific and production assets, ensuring that valuable content is preserved and easily retrievable. 4. Immersive Media Fellow Immersive Media Fellows explore cutting-edge technologies such as virtual reality (VR), augmented reality (AR), game design, and user experience (UX/UI). Their goal is to create interactive experiences that bring ocean exploration and education to life for diverse audiences. 5. Marketing and Communications Fellow This track provides experience in planning and executing strategic communication campaigns. Fellows contribute to content creation, digital outreach, and cross-channel marketing initiatives while collaborating with various teams across the organization. 6. Partnerships Fellow Partnerships Fellows play a key role in building and maintaining relationships with stakeholders. This includes identifying potential collaborators, supporting ongoing partnerships, and contributing to strategic growth initiatives. Program Timeline, Structure, and Location Key Dates March 24, 2026 – Applications open May 3, 2026 (11:59 PM ET) – Application deadline Mid-May 2026 – Candidate interviews Late May 2026 – Fellowship offers announced June 22, 2026 – Fellowship begins with onboarding December 2026 – Fellowship concludes Remote Format and Work Expectations The fellowship is conducted entirely remotely, allowing participants to work from their home countries. Despite its remote structure, the program is highly interactive and requires active participation in virtual training sessions, mentorship meetings, and collaborative projects. Fellows are expected to align their working hours with Singapore Time (SGT) to facilitate coordination with OceanX teams. However, candidates in the Marketing and Communications track may have the flexibility to operate within either Singapore or New York (Eastern Time) business hours, depending on their location. Financial Support and Benefits Fellows receive a monthly stipend intended to support their full-time engagement in the program. It is important to note that this stipend does not cover additional expenses such as housing, relocation, transportation, or dependent care. In certain cases, a technology stipend may be provided to support specific needs related to the fellowship. Additionally, the program offers intangible benefits including mentorship, global networking opportunities, and exposure to real-world ocean exploration initiatives. Why the Ocean Futures Fellowship Matters The Ocean Futures Fellowship stands out as a multidisciplinary program that bridges science, technology, storytelling, and policy. By combining professional training with real-world application, it prepares participants to become influential voices in ocean conservation and innovation. Through its alignment with global initiatives and its emphasis on emerging technologies such as AI and immersive media, the fellowship reflects the evolving nature of ocean careers. It not only builds technical expertise but also cultivates leadership, creativity, and a strong sense of global responsibility. For individuals passionate about the future of the oceans and eager to contribute to meaningful change, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to develop skills, expand networks, and engage with one of the most dynamic fields in the world today. Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Ocean Futures Fellowship, applicants must meet several requirements: Be at least 21 years old Be authorized to work in their country of residence Be available to commit full-time from June to December 2026 Hold a bachelor’s degree from an accredited institution before the fellowship begins Demonstrate experience or strong interest in one of the six fellowship tracks Possess full professional proficiency in English (C2 level or equivalent) Have practical knowledge of AI literacy and a commitment to using emerging technologies responsibly Show strong motivation for purpose-driven work, mentorship, and global collaboration Additional academic requirements apply to specific tracks: Partnerships Fellows should have backgrounds in fields such as finance, policy, science, nonprofit management, business, or communications Immersive Media and Digital Correspondent Fellows should have academic training in ocean science, conservation, media, or related disciplines Application Process Applicants must complete an online application that includes multiple components designed to assess both technical qualifications and personal motivation. These include: General background information and work experience related to ocean education or relevant fields A current resume or CV Written responses to short essay questions A short video explaining the applicant’s qualifications and reasons for applying Before starting the application, candidates should prepare the following: A link to their resume/CV (required) A link to their video response (required) Optional links to sample projects and proof of English proficiency CLICK HERE TO APPLY Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply for OceanX Ocean Futures Fellowship 2026 (Paid Remote Program for Ocean Professionals) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Nova Media Fellowship 2026–2027 ($60K for Journalists and Newsrooms)
summary: The Nova Media Fellowship 2026–2027 is a prestigious, fully funded program designed to support exceptional journalists and nonprofit newsrooms reporting on the complex, interconnected factors that shape health and well-being. This fellowship empowers storytellers to produce impactful, evidence-based, and solution-focused journalism that advances public understanding and inspires meaningful change. Each year, Nova selects a small cohort of Media Fellows whose work explores pressing global issues related to human flourishing. Past fellows have published in leading outlets such as The New York Times, The Guardian, Science, Bloomberg, NPR, and Grist , among others—demonstrating the fellowship’s strong reputation and reach. About the Nova Media Fellowship The Nova Media Fellowship provides selected journalists with the time, funding, and editorial freedom needed to pursue ambitious reporting projects. Fellows maintain full editorial independence , allowing them to define their storytelling approach, angles, and dissemination strategies. The program emphasizes: Evidence-based journalism Solution-oriented storytelling Broad audience engagement across sectors (science, policy, communities, and the public) For the 2026–2027 cohort , Nova will select three outstanding fellows . Fellowship Benefits Selected Media Fellows will receive: $60,000 grant distributed over 12 months Additional travel and conference stipends Flexible support to explore in-depth and evolving storylines Opportunities to reach global audiences and decision-makers Fellowship Duration: September 1, 2026 – August 31, 2027 Why Apply? The Nova Media Fellowship is more than just funding—it’s an opportunity to: Produce high-impact journalism that influences global conversations Explore complex health challenges through innovative lenses Contribute to a future where all people can thrive and flourish If you are a journalist passionate about uncovering solutions and telling stories that matter, this fellowship offers the platform, resources, and freedom to bring your ideas to life. Eligibility Criteria To apply for the Nova Media Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Who Can Apply Professional journalists with a proven track record of publication (local, regional, or national) Nonprofit newsrooms Student-led newsrooms affiliated with educational institutions Key Requirements Applicants must reside in the United States throughout the fellowship period Projects may include international or global reporting Not Eligible Book proposals Poetry, songwriting, or artwork Academic research projects Nova strongly encourages applications from individuals and communities historically underrepresented in media, including: Women People of color LGBTQ+ individuals Indigenous and Native communities Fellowship Focus Areas (2026–2027) Nova is particularly interested in proposals addressing innovative and emerging themes, including: 1. Children’s Health and Development Nutrition, education, and early childhood experiences Upstream factors shaping lifelong well-being 2. Mental Health and Justice Legal systems and the “legalome” Compassionate and equitable justice frameworks 3. Planetary Health and Collective Well-Being Climate, ecology, and interconnected global systems Practices that foster connection with the natural world 4. AI, Technology, and Society Impacts of artificial intelligence on healthcare and mental health Broader societal implications of emerging technologies 5. Health System Innovation New models of primary healthcare Addressing systemic inequities 6. Food Systems and Regenerative Agriculture Sustainable agriculture practices Transformative food systems Note: These themes are not exhaustive. Nova welcomes creative, interdisciplinary proposals that explore new ways of understanding health and human flourishing. What Makes a Strong Application? Successful proposals should: Go beyond identifying problems to highlight innovative solutions Demonstrate a clear reporting plan and timeline Include a strategy for reaching broad audiences Provide a publication and dissemination roadmap , including: Target outlets Social media strategies Newsletters (e.g., Substack) Speaking engagements Applicants are also encouraged to include: A travel budget Existing editorial partnerships or contacts A realistic set of deliverables for a one-year project Application Requirements For Freelance Journalists: Written project proposal Résumé Relevant work samples For Newsrooms: Application completed by a senior representative (e.g., managing editor) Organizational details including: Structure Editorial focus Available resources Finalists: Must submit two professional letters of recommendation Participate in virtual panel interviews CLICK HERE TO REGISTER Application Timeline Applications Open: Friday, March 20, 2026 Info Session (Zoom): Thursday, April 2, 2026 (4:00 PM ET) Application Deadline: Monday, April 20, 2026 (11:59 PM ET) Semi-finalist Interviews: Mid-May 2026 Finalist Interviews: Mid-June 2026 Fellowship Awards Announced: Early July 2026 Program Start Date: September 1, 2026 Note: Dates (except the deadline) may be subject to change. Additional Requirements and Expectations Fellows must credit Nova’s support in all published work related to the fellowship Nova affiliation should be included in bios and bylines , even for unrelated work during the fellowship term Fellows are encouraged to embrace curiosity-driven exploration , following new leads and evolving narratives Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: Nova Media Fellowship 2026–2027 ($60K for Journalists and Newsrooms) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GROW – Global Research Opportunities for the South (DAAD 2026–2027): Fully Funded Research in Germany
summary: The GROW – Global Research Opportunities for the South Programme , funded by the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD), offers fully funded short-term research opportunities in Germany for scholars from developing countries. Supported by the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), this programme strengthens international research collaboration and promotes development-oriented research aligned with global priorities. Overview of the GROW Programme The GROW programme is designed to support: Doctoral candidates Postdoctoral researchers University lecturers and professors Established academics and scientists Eligible applicants must be based in countries listed on the OECD DAC list—primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa , with additional opportunities for scholars in Latin America and Asia . Key Objective The programme aims to: Support development-focused research projects Strengthen international academic collaboration Build scientific capacity in home countries Promote research aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Participants will gain advanced research skills, expand global networks, and contribute to institutional development upon returning home. Programme Structure The GROW programme offers two funding tracks: 1. Preparatory and Cooperation Visits (Programme Line 1) Duration: 1–2 months Purpose: Develop joint research ideas Build academic partnerships Prepare publications and funding proposals Focus: Establishing long-term research collaborations 2. Research Stays (Programme Line 2) Duration: 2–6 months Purpose: Conduct an independent research project in Germany Work with a host academic supervisor Develop a clear transfer plan for applying results in your home country What Does the Scholarship Cover? The DAAD GROW programme provides comprehensive financial support, including: Monthly stipend: €1,400 (doctoral candidates postdocs) €2,000 (assistant professors/lecturers) €2,150 (full professors) Travel allowance Health, accident, and liability insurance Additional support (where applicable): Disability or chronic illness-related costs payments towards health, accident and personal liability insurance cover (see also our important information for scholarship applicants / section F, point 4) travel allowance Under certain circumstances, you can apply for the following additional benefits after start of funding: In case of a disability or chronic illness: on application, a subsidy may be provided for justified additional costs incurred abroad that are necessary to realise the project in Germany and that are not covered by a third party; whether and to what extent a subsidy will be paid, will be reviewed and determined on an individual basis (see important information for scholarship applicants , section F, point 8) Benefits from third parties are partially credited towards the DAAD scholarship. Information on this can be found in the important scholarship information in section F, point 6. Eligibility Criteria Please check if the following criteria are fulfilled (also read our important scholarship information , section A): To apply, you must: Be employed at a university or research institute in your home country Be from an OECD DAC-listed country. Applications are open to doctoral candidates, postdoctoral researchers, university teachers and established academics and scientists from countries on the OECD DAC list—primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa, but also in Latin America and Asia , who are employed at a university or a research institute in their home country. Meet academic stage requirements: Doctoral students: PhD started within the last 3 years Postdocs: PhD completed within the last 4 years Not have lived in Germany for more than 15 months at the time of application Not Eligible If: You are already based in Germany at the time of application Your entire research is being conducted at a German institution Note: Women and candidates from disadvantaged backgrounds are strongly encouraged to apply. Research Focus: SDG-Aligned Projects All proposals must demonstrate a strong link to at least one Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) , such as: Climate action Health systems Education Governance Social inclusion Innovation systems Projects should clearly show development relevance and potential for real-world impact . Duration and Timeline Programme must start in 2026 All activities must be completed by 31 January 2027 Scholarships cannot be extended Selection Criteria Applications are evaluated by an independent academic committee based on: Academic Excellence Qualifications, grades, and publications Quality of Proposal Relevance to development challenges Alignment with SDGs Feasibility and clarity of research plan Impact Sustainability Potential to strengthen institutions in the home country Long-term collaboration prospects Knowledge transfer and capacity building Motivation Career Potential Academic and professional relevance Commitment to development impact The selection committee also gives due consideration to equal opportunities ; you can provide relevant information in the application form (see important scholarship information , section E, point 3). For more information on the selection procedure, go to important scholarship information , section E. Application Deadline 📅 30 April 2026 📢 Selection Results: June 2026 🚀 Programme Start: August 2026 Required Application Documents For Programme Line 1: online application form curriculum vitae (max. 2 pages): complete, in tabular form detailed description of the project justifying the purpose and objectives of the stay (max. 1–2 pages): Please outline the objectives pursued with the preparatory/cooperation visit and explain how the results achieved and the cooperations initiated are expected, after your return, to contribute to the strengthening of scientific structures and to the promotion of early-career researchers in your country of origin. Please also refer to the specific development challenges in your home country addressed by the project (including reference to the relevant Sustainable Development Goal(s) (SDGs). clearly structured time schedule and itinerary (host institutions / academic hosts) of the planned research stay (to be uploaded in the portal under “Schedule”) written confirmation from the academic host in Germany ; the confirmation must explicitly refer to your project (including the working language) and confirm that a workplace will be provided by the host institution in Germany proof of affiliation with a higher education institution in your home country (e.g. proof of employment, admission to doctoral studies, certificate of enrolment, etc.) Declaration from the scientific supervisor or host in Germany on safety-relevant and ethical aspects of the research project. Please upload the document in addition to the confirmation from the academic host in the portal under ‘Scientific ethics/research safety ‘. For Programme Line 2: online application form curriculum vitae (max. 2 pages): complete, in tabular form list of publications (max. 2 pages) of your most important published work letter of motivation (max. 1 page): statement about the academic and personal reasons for the planned research project in Germany, taking account of the following question: What do you hope to gain from your project in Germany (personally, professionally, for your career)? Please upload the letter of motivation under study project/motivation in the DAAD portal. Proposal including a transfer concept (total max. 5 pages): Please submit a detailed and precise description (independently prepared and written by yourself) of your research project, which has been coordinated with the academic host in Germany. Please explain the relevance of the project to specific development challenges (including reference to the relevant SDG(s)) addressed by the project. Furthermore, please describe, by means of concrete measures, how you will use the knowledge and contacts gained after your return to contribute to scientific and institutional capacity building in your country of origin (transfer concept). Please also read our important scholarship information / section B, point 2. Please upload the proposal under research project in the DAAD portal. time schedule of planned research work confirmation from the academic host in Germany ; the confirmation must refer to your project and guarantee that the host institute in Germany will provide you with a workplace. Also read our important scholarship information / section C, point 3. Please upload the confirmation from the academic host under acceptance of host institution in the DAAD portal. Declaration from the scientific supervisor or host in Germany on safety-relevant and ethical aspects of the research project. Please upload the document in addition to the confirmation from the academic host in the portal under ‘Scientific ethics/research safety ‘. Doctoral Candidates and Postdoctoral Researchers must additionally submit: university degree certificates (e.g. Master s, doctorate) stating your final grade(s); please submit only the most recent degree certificate. If you do not have your most recent degree certificate (doctoral candidates: Master s; postdocs: doctorate) at the time of application, you must submit it before the start of your scholarship. Please upload your certificate(s) under university degree certificates in the DAAD portal. Proof of admission to doctoral studies / enrolment in a doctoral programme / letter of supervision from a faculty member at the home university (doctoral candidates only). Please upload the document in the portal under “Admission” One recent supporting letter of recommendation (on a DAAD-form) from a university teacher (for doctoral students, from the supervisor of your doctoral thesis) which provides information about your qualifications. Please regard the instructions given on the tab Submitting an application . Applications must be submitted online via the DAAD Portal. Please note that the access to the application portal only appears while the current application period is running. After the application deadline has expired, the portal for this programme is not available until the next application period. Please note Your application is only valid if you submit all the required documents to the DAAD portal by the application deadline. The DAAD portal closes at 24.00 hrs. (CET or CEST) on the last application day. If possible, please do not send your application on the final date in case technical problems occur. Incomplete applications cannot be considered. Applicants are responsible for ensuring that their applications are complete. The application documents remain with the DAAD. Data relating to applicants is saved by the DAAD in accordance with the Federal Data Protection Act and with the EU Data Protection Regulation insofar as this data is needed to process the application. How to Apply Applications must be submitted via the DAAD online portal . Steps: Register on the DAAD portal Request recommendation letter Upload all required documents (PDF format) Submit application before the deadline Important Notes: Incomplete applications will not be considered Submit early to avoid technical issues Documents in other languages must include English or German translations Why Apply for DAAD GROW? Fully funded research experience in Germany Access to world-class universities and research institutions Opportunity to contribute to global development challenges Build long-term international academic networks Strengthen your career and institutional impact APPLY OR FIND MORE INFO HERE Contact Information Information and advisory centres DAAD Section ST32 Sub-Saharan Africa Kennedyallee 50 D-53175 Bonn Contact: st32@daad.de More detailed information DAAD website Important information for DAAD scholarship applicants DAAD website section Doing a PhD and Research in Germany Website Research in Germany Website GERiT – German Research Institutions The DAAD GROW Programme 2026–2027 is a highly competitive and prestigious opportunity for researchers committed to solving real-world development challenges. If you are passionate about research with impact and global collaboration, this programme offers the funding, network, and platform to take your work to the next level. General information on the application via DAAD portal Click on Application portal at the bottom of the page to go to the DAAD portal. There you will be provided with an online application form to enter your application data. This is what you have to do: 1. Register in the DAAD portal ( Read notes about registering in the portal ) 2. Request recommendation form You can generate the recommendation form in the DAAD Portal as a writable PDF under Request recommendation form . It can only be generated during the application period. Please send it to your university teacher and ask him or her to complete the form in full. When you have received it back, upload it in the portal under Letter of Recommendation . 3. Applying online in the DAAD portal ( Read notes on applying in the portal ) Download and complete the online application form Prepare application documents If necessary, translate documents (unless they are already in German or English). Scan paper documents and save in PDF format. Please note that you can only upload PDF files to the portal. Upload the completed application form and the other application documents to the portal in PDF format. Translations, if applicable, should be uploaded together with the document issued in the original language. Submit the uploaded application documents online. Please note: If you have any technical questions or problems your local information and advice centres could not help you with, please do not hesitate to contact portal@daad.de via e-mail. You can also call the technical portal hotline on weekdays from 9-12 and 14-16 (CET/CEST) on +49 (0) 228 882-8888. Please consider possible processing times of your request when you are planning your application. GROW – Global Research Opportunities for the South Programm Line 1 To the application portal » GROW – Global Research Opportunities for the South Programme Line 2 To the application portal » APPLY OR FIND MORE INFO HERE The post GROW – Global Research Opportunities for the South (DAAD 2026–2027): Fully Funded Research in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) 2026–2027: Fully Funded Continental Youth Leadership Programme | 50 Positions Available Across Africa
summary: The African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) is now open for applications for the 2026–2027 cohort. This prestigious, fully funded programme offers young African professionals the opportunity to serve as volunteers across the continent while gaining hands-on experience in policy, development, and leadership. If you are passionate about Africa’s development and want to contribute to Agenda 2063: “The Africa We Want” , this is one of the most competitive and impactful youth opportunities available. About the African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) Established in 2010, the AU-YVC is a flagship youth leadership initiative designed to: Recruit, train, and deploy young African professionals Promote youth participation in continental development Strengthen skills, leadership, and cross-cultural collaboration Volunteers are deployed for 12 months in another AU Member State, contributing to development programmes and policy initiatives. Programme Overview Programme: African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) Organization: African Union Commission Duration: 12 months Number of Positions: 50 Start Date: 2026/2027 cycle Application Deadline: April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EAT) Key Objectives The AU-YVC programme aims to: Empower young Africans as drivers of development Promote volunteerism across the continent Enhance youth engagement in policy and governance Foster a more integrated, peaceful, and prosperous Africa Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be a citizen of an AU Member State (living in Africa or the diaspora) Be aged 18–35 years Hold at least a post-secondary qualification (TVET, Bachelor’s, Master’s, etc.) Be available for 12 months of full-time volunteer service Be willing to live and work in another African country Be proficient in at least one AU working language (Arabic, English, French, Kiswahili, Portuguese, or Spanish) Have: At least 1 year of volunteering experience At least 1 year of professional work experience Required Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Strong computer skills (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint) Proficiency in email and internet tools Excellent communication (written and verbal) Strong interpersonal and teamwork skills Multilingual ability (an added advantage) Core Competencies Successful candidates are expected to: Communicate clearly and effectively Write concisely and professionally Work in multicultural environments Demonstrate integrity and high ethical standards Show commitment and professionalism Volunteer Benefits and Entitlements Selected AU Youth Volunteers will receive: Full pre-deployment training Economy return ticket to duty station Monthly stipend for living expenses Health insurance coverage AU Service Passport Separation allowance upon completion Access to training and mentorship opportunities Opportunity to engage in high-level continental policy work Networking with young leaders across Africa Application Process (Step-by-Step) 1. Complete Your Profile Visit the AU careers portal and: Register or log in Fill in your personal information 2. Upload Required Documents Updated CV ( maximum 3 pages ) Passport bio page or national ID Highest qualification certificate (no transcripts) Recommendation letter (not older than 2 years) 3. Submit a Cover Letter Your cover letter must answer: Why do you want to become an AU Youth Volunteer? What makes you the best candidate? What skills will you bring to the programme? 4. Submit Before Deadline Ensure your application is submitted by: April 10, 2026 (11:59 PM EAT) ⚠️ Mandatory Requirement: AU CV Template All applicants must use the official African Union CV template . Applications without the AU CV format will NOT be considered Download, complete, and upload the template before applying Important Notes Incomplete or late applications will not be accepted Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted Providing false or incomplete information may lead to disqualification The African Union does NOT charge any application or facilitation fees Why You Should Apply The AU-YVC is more than a volunteer programme—it is a career-defining opportunity to: Gain continental exposure Build leadership and professional skills Contribute directly to Africa’s development agenda Join a network of future African leaders The African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) is one of the most impactful youth programmes in Africa, offering a unique platform for young professionals to drive change at a continental level. If you meet the criteria and are ready to make a difference, apply before the deadline and take your place among Africa’s next generation of leaders . FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE More opportunities for Africans: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=Africa The post African Union Youth Volunteer Corps (AU-YVC) 2026–2027: Fully Funded Continental Youth Leadership Programme | 50 Positions Available Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Knauss Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Ocean Policy Program in the USA
summary: The Knauss Fellowship is a prestigious, one-year program that offers graduate students the opportunity to apply their academic training to real-world policy challenges related to oceans, coasts, and the Great Lakes. Hosted by the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) through its Sea Grant program, this fellowship places participants in high-impact roles within the U.S. federal government. Fellows gain hands-on experience in policy-making while contributing scientific knowledge, innovation, and fresh perspectives to national decision-making processes. In return, they develop practical skills, expand their professional networks, and prepare for leadership careers at the intersection of science and public policy. Fellowship Overview The Knauss Fellowship provides a unique pathway from academia to government, allowing fellows to engage directly with policy development and implementation. Duration One year (June 2027 – May 2028) Location Washington, D.C. metropolitan area Placement Opportunities Fellows are placed in: Federal executive branch offices Legislative branch offices (e.g., Congress) Areas of Work Participants contribute to a wide range of policy-related activities, including: Policy analysis and development Science communication Program evaluation Stakeholder engagement Environmental and marine resource management Application Details Application Status: Open Deadline: June 3, 2026 Application Process: Applicants must apply through their state Sea Grant program , which reviews submissions and nominates candidates for the national competition. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Knauss Fellowship, applicants must: Be enrolled in a graduate program (Master’s, PhD, or JD) at an accredited university in the United States or its territories at the time of application Demonstrate a strong interest in public service and marine or coastal policy Be a U.S. citizen, national, or dual citizen Fellowship Impact and Reach The Knauss Fellowship has a long-standing track record of developing future leaders in marine policy and public service: 47 years of program success 1,762 fellows placed in government roles 69 host offices across the U.S. government (average between 2022–2025) This extensive reach highlights the fellowship’s role in shaping policy professionals and advancing science-informed decision-making. Opportunities for Different Participants Prospective Fellows Graduate students interested in marine and environmental policy can apply through their state Sea Grant programs and compete for national placement. Current Fellows Alumni Participants gain access to essential career resources and a strong alumni network that supports long-term professional growth. Prospective Hosts Government offices interested in hosting fellows in future cycles (e.g., 2027) will have opportunities to participate and benefit from the program’s talent pool. Recent Knauss Fellowship Updates Recent announcements from the Sea Grant program highlight the continued growth and competitiveness of the fellowship: 2026: Announcement of the latest fellowship cohorts selected for national programs 2024–2025: Finalists and fellows recognized for their contributions to marine policy and leadership development These updates reflect the program’s ongoing commitment to bridging science and policy through practical, government-based experience. Why Apply for the Knauss Fellowship? The Knauss Fellowship stands out as a premier opportunity for graduate students seeking careers in environmental policy and public service. It offers: Direct exposure to federal policy-making Hands-on experience in executive and legislative offices Opportunities to influence real-world environmental decisions A strong national professional network Career advancement in science-policy leadership How to Apply To begin your application: Locate your state Sea Grant program Review the Notice of Funding Opportunity Submit your application through your state program before the deadline The Knauss Fellowship is more than a fellowship—it is a career-launching platform for future leaders committed to shaping policies that protect and sustain marine and coastal ecosystems. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply for the Knauss Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Ocean Policy Program in the USA appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Dissemination Grant for Local Government Associations 2026: Funding Opportunity Up to SEK 150,000 to Strengthen Democratic Governance in Africa
summary: The International Centre for Local Democracy (ICLD) has officially launched a Call for Proposals for its Dissemination Grant to Local Government Associations (2026) —a unique funding opportunity designed to empower organizations working with municipalities to strengthen democratic governance at the local level. This grant represents a strategic investment in capacity-building, knowledge transfer, and institutional strengthening , particularly across selected African countries. It is tailored for organizations that play a key role in supporting, training, or representing local governments and are committed to enhancing governance practices through practical tools and training initiatives. Overview of the Dissemination Grant The ICLD Dissemination Grant provides financial support of up to SEK 150,000 (including VAT) to eligible organizations. The aim is to facilitate the design and implementation of training initiatives targeting local government representatives, using ICLD’s well-established democratic governance tools. Unlike general funding schemes, this grant is highly focused: it emphasizes practical application , ensuring that local governments not only receive training but also actively integrate governance tools into their policies, processes, and institutional frameworks. Purpose and Strategic Objectives At its core, this grant is designed to bridge the gap between knowledge and implementation . Organizations that have participated in ICLD’s Training of Trainers on Democratic Governance Tools are especially encouraged to apply, as the grant enables them to disseminate acquired knowledge within their national or regional contexts. The key objectives include: Strengthening local democratic institutions Promoting good governance practices Enhancing capacity of municipalities and local officials Facilitating practical application of governance tools Supporting peer-to-peer learning and institutional development By funding dissemination activities, ICLD ensures that its training programs have a multiplier effect , reaching a broader network of local governments. Scope of the Grant The Dissemination Grant must be used to plan, organize, and implement a structured training initiative for local government representatives. Eligible activities include: Organizing training workshops or seminars Developing training materials based on ICLD tools Facilitating sessions for elected officials and public servants Supporting municipalities in applying governance tools in real contexts The proposed training must: Target local-level elected officials and/or public officials Use one or more ICLD democratic governance tools Enable municipalities to apply these tools in practice Align with ICLD’s Code of Conduct and principles Importantly, the applying organization is fully responsible for all aspects of implementation, including logistics, financial management, and reporting. Sub-granting or transferring funds to other entities is strictly prohibited. Eligible Countries This opportunity is specifically open to organizations operating in the following countries: South Africa Botswana Zambia Zimbabwe Uganda Kenya Tanzania Grant Value and Financial Conditions Applicants can request funding of up to SEK 150,000 (including VAT) . The disbursement structure is as follows: Up to 75% of the grant is paid in advance Remaining 25% is released after: Completion of activities Submission of final narrative and financial reports Approval by ICLD Eligible expenses include: Personnel and staff time Training and conference costs (venue, printing, meals) Travel and accommodation for participants Organizations must demonstrate strong financial management capacity , maintain documentation (invoices, receipts, contracts), and ensure transparency in all expenditures. Implementation Timeline Applicants must adhere to a strict timeline: Application Deadline: 10 May 2026 Notification of Results: 25 May 2026 Implementation Period: June – August 2026 Final Reporting Deadline: 31 August 2026 All activities must be completed and reported by the end of August 2026 to receive the full grant amount. Deliverables and Reporting Requirements Successful applicants are required to submit: Final Narrative Report Overview of the training conducted Participating municipalities Outcomes and results Lessons learned Financial Report Detailed breakdown of all expenses Comparison with the approved budget Failure to meet reporting requirements may result in delayed or reduced funding. Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must: Be a legally registered organization Work in training, supporting, or representing local governments Have experience in capacity development Demonstrate financial transparency and accountability Be capable of managing and completing the project within the timeline Priority will be given to: Associations of Local Governments Organizations that participated in ICLD’s Training of Trainers (March–April 2026) Note: Individual applications are not accepted, and only one application per organization is allowed. Evaluation and Selection Process Applications will be assessed based on: Relevance to local governance needs Quality and clarity of the training design Understanding and application of ICLD tools Organizational capacity and experience Feasibility within the given timeframe Cost-effectiveness of the proposed budget ICLD aims to balance breadth (number of local governments reached) and depth (quality of learning and implementation) . How to Apply Applicants must submit a proposal (maximum 5 pages, excluding budget) in Word format. The proposal should include: Organizational background and capacity Description of proposed activities Target group and number of participants Selected ICLD tools and application strategy Expected outcomes Timeline Detailed budget (max SEK 150,000) 📧 Submit applications via email to: kc@icld.se 📅 Deadline: 10 May 2026 Why This Opportunity Matters This grant is more than just funding—it is a platform for impact . It allows organizations to: Scale their expertise Influence governance at the grassroots level Strengthen democratic practices across municipalities Build long-term institutional capacity For organizations working in local governance, this is a high-impact opportunity to translate knowledge into real-world change. For more information click here APPLY HERE Discover more information here The post Dissemination Grant for Local Government Associations 2026: Funding Opportunity Up to SEK 150,000 to Strengthen Democratic Governance in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship
summary: The MPhil/PhD Studentship – Pan-African Frontiers: Reparative Futures and Global Order at SOAS University of London offers a fully funded doctoral opportunity for candidates interested in Pan-Africanism, global justice, reparations, and international governance . This studentship is part of a UKRI Future Leaders Fellowship project led by Daniel Mulugeta and hosted at the SOAS Centre for Pan-African Studies (CPAS) within the Department of Politics and International Studies. Key Information Host Institution: SOAS University of London Programme: Full-time MPhil/PhD in Politics and International Studies Academic Year: 2026/27 Funding: Full tuition fees plus maintenance stipend Minimum Stipend: £20,622 (2026/27 estimate) Duration: 3 years (full-time) Number of Awards: 1 Deadline: 15 May 2026 (12 noon UK time) Scholarship Overview The Pan-African Frontiers project explores Pan-Africanism as a transcontinental political formation linking Africa, the Caribbean, and diasporic communities. It focuses on: Global justice and reparations Historical redress for slavery and colonialism Institutional reform and global governance New forms of political agency and solidarity The research programme brings together scholars and practitioners across Africa, the Caribbean, Europe, and North America to examine how historically marginalised communities are shaping the future of global order . Scholarship Benefits Full tuition fees (Home and Overseas applicants) Maintenance stipend (minimum £20,622) Three years of doctoral funding Teaching experience through the SOAS Teaching Scholarship scheme Access to interdisciplinary research networks and global collaboration Research Areas Applicants should propose projects aligned with themes such as: Reparations and historical justice Pan-Africanism and global political order Diaspora engagement and transnational networks African and Caribbean participation in global governance Multilateralism and institutional reform Regional cooperation in Africa and the Caribbean Political responses to global inequality and structural injustice Projects may adopt political, sociological, historical, or interdisciplinary approaches and may focus on continental, regional, or diasporic contexts. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Apply for a new MPhil/PhD programme starting in 2026/27 Hold or expect a Master’s degree with merit or distinction (or international equivalent) Meet SOAS English language requirements (if applicable) Be eligible for Home or Overseas fee status Required Documents Applications must include: Personal statement (maximum 1 page) Research proposal (maximum 4 pages) CV (maximum 2 pages) One academic reference Academic transcripts and certificates Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable) Incomplete applications will not be considered. How to Apply Step 1: Apply for the Programme All applications must include the following documents. Applications that do not contain these documents will not be considered. Personal statement explaining your motivation for applying and how your proposed research relates to the themes of the project (maximum 1 page). Research proposal outlining the proposed doctoral project (maximum 4 pages). Curriculum vitae (CV) including relevant academic background and experience (maximum 2 pages). One academic reference. Academic transcripts and degree certificates. Proof of English language proficiency (if applicable), such as IELTS or equivalent. Step 2: Apply for the Scholarship The deadline for application is 12 noon UK local time on 15 May 2026. You must apply for this scholarship via the admissions portal . You will need to have applied for a course to get login credentials for this. If you do not see the scholarship you are hoping to apply for within your admissions portal, please double-check you meet the eligibility criteria. Should you have any queries, please contact scholarships@soas.ac.uk . Selection Process Applications will be assessed based on: Academic merit Quality and relevance of research proposal Personal statement Relevant academic or professional experience Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. Notification of Results Shortlisting and selection expected from June 2026 Successful candidates contacted first All applicants notified once the process is complete Contact Information Programme enquiries: dsadmissions@soas.ac.uk Scholarship enquiries: scholarships@soas.ac.uk Why This Opportunity Matters This fully funded PhD offers a strong platform for candidates seeking to contribute to global debates on justice, reparations, and institutional reform . It provides the opportunity to work within a leading research environment and engage with critical issues shaping Africa, the diaspora, and the future of global governance . Source: https://www.soas.ac.uk/study/student-life/finance/scholarships/mphil/phd-studentship-pan-african-frontiers-reparative The post MPhil/PhD Studentship 2026 at SOAS University of London – Fully Funded Pan-African Frontiers Scholarship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth)
summary: The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 is a global, paid United Nations Volunteer initiative designed to expand access to employment opportunities for refugee, asylum-seeking, internally displaced, and migrant youth. Hosted by UNICEF , the programme provides a structured pathway for young people to gain professional experience and contribute meaningfully to international development. Overview of the Programme The Youth on the Move Programme offers a 12-month paid UN Volunteer assignment within UNICEF country offices. It is specifically designed to address systemic barriers that prevent displaced and migrant youth from accessing internships and entry-level roles. The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme builds on the success of the Youth on the Move Fellowship Pilot Programme —implemented by UNICEF in partnership with the Migration Youth and Children Platform. This pilot included 23 young people from refugee and displaced backgrounds to work with UNICEF between 2022 and 2024 (see the video ). While the 2024–2025 edition engaged 13 UN Volunteers with UNICEF in Cambodia, Côte d’Ivoire, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Mozambique, Poland, Somalia, Tanzania, Trinidad and Tobago, and Vietnam. The 2026 edition coincides with the International Volunteer Year (IVY) 2026 and builds on the momentum of previous cohorts. Key Benefits Participants in the programme will receive: Paid 12-month UN Volunteer placement Professional experience within UNICEF country offices Exposure to international development and humanitarian work Opportunities to build networks within the UN system Career development support and practical work experience Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Aged 20–32 years Refugees, asylum seekers, internally displaced persons, or migrants Facing barriers to education-to-employment pathways due to displacement or migration Based in the country of assignment Legally eligible to work in the country of assignment Additional considerations: UNICEF promotes equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply Youth with disabilities are encouraged to apply UNICEF is committed to equity, non-discrimination, and inclusive recruitment, particularly for youth on the move, including those with disabilities. Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply to support gender parity and address the heightened vulnerabilities faced by women and girls in displaced communities (see link ). Available Opportunities (2026) Current programme locations include: Uganda Sudan More opportunities may be added as the programme expands. Application Process To apply for the UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026: Register on the UN Volunteer Unified Platform Complete your profile and required documentation Review available roles and apply directly Consult guidance and application resources provided on the platform To join the 31 March information webinar on the cohort , please click here . How to apply? Visit the Unified Volunteer Platform or apply to the positions below. Register your profile by c licking on guidance and resources . Programme Vision The programme is grounded in the principle that talent is universal, but access to opportunity is not. It aims to address systemic exclusion faced by displaced youth by creating equitable access to employment pathways. Core objectives include: Breaking barriers to entry into international organizations Promoting inclusion and representation within global institutions Unlocking untapped potential among displaced and migrant youth Supporting sustainable development through youth participation The UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 represents a significant opportunity for young people affected by displacement to gain paid, meaningful work experience within the United Nations system. It is both a career pathway and a strategic intervention aimed at advancing equity in global employment opportunities. CLICK FOR MORE INFO AND TO APPLY The post UNICEF UNV Youth on the Move Programme 2026 (Paid UN Volunteer Opportunity for Refugee and Migrant Youth) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions
summary: Applications are now open for HerCyberTracks 2026 , a high-impact international programme designed to empower women professionals in cybersecurity with advanced skills, global exposure, and leadership opportunities. This initiative is funded by the Federal Foreign Office of Germany and the European Union , and co-implemented by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in partnership with the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime (UNODC) and the Latin America and Caribbean Cyber Competence Centre (LAC4) . About the Programme HerCyberTracks is a five-month blended learning programme that combines virtual training, self-paced learning, mentorship, and in-person regional sessions. It is designed to support women working in cybersecurity-related roles to accelerate their careers, deepen technical expertise, and build strong international networks . Programme Structure Participants will engage in: Live online sessions with industry experts Self-paced learning modules In-person regional training sessions Personalised mentorship Inspirational webinars and keynote sessions This hybrid format ensures both flexibility and immersive learning experiences. Specialised Tracks Applicants can choose one of four tailored tracks: Policy and Diplomacy Incident Response Criminal Justice Cyber or AI Each track is designed to provide practical knowledge and leadership skills relevant to the evolving cybersecurity landscape. What You’ll Gain Participants will benefit from: Advanced cybersecurity and policy skills Access to global experts and mentors International networking opportunities Exposure to cross-regional collaboration Leadership development to influence policy and systems Who Should Apply This programme targets women professionals working in: Public sector institutions Critical infrastructure organisations Cybersecurity, policy, or related technical fields Eligible Regions Applications are open to candidates from: African Union member states Western Balkans Eastern Partnership countries (selected) Latin America and the Caribbean Indo-Pacific region Programme Duration Duration: 5 months (2026 cohort) Application Deadline Applications close on April 19, 2026 . How to Apply Apply here. For more Training Programs from OFY, Click here. The post HerCyberTracks 2026: Global Cybersecurity Programme for Women Professionals Across Multiple Regions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
